title: The Product Hunt Graveyard: 1 in 4 Top Launches (2023–2025) Is Already Dead
excerpt: As of July 18, 2026: 24.4% of 2,291 top Product Hunt launches from 2023–2025 no longer exist — dead domains, parked pages, expired certificates. AI tools die at the same rate as everything else (24.5% vs 24.4%), but a top-5 launch-day finish cuts the death rate to 9%. Full dataset downloadable, evidence per product.
content_html:
State of AI Tools/The Graveyard

Original research by RightAIChoice · every website re-probed, verdicts as of July 18, 2026 · every verdict carries downloadable evidence

TL;DR — as of July 18, 2026

  • We visited the live website of 2,291 productsthat made Product Hunt’s daily leaderboards between January 2023 and April 2025.
  • 24.4% are hard-dead — the domain no longer resolves, the page is parked for sale, the security certificate expired, or the homepage returns 404.
  • Another 4.5% now redirect to a different company (acquired or quietly folded). Total gone as independent products: 28.9%.
  • AI tools die at the same rate as everything else — 24.5% vs 24.4%. The AI boom changed what gets built, not what survives.
  • Launch-day success is real protection: finish in your day’s top 5 and the death rate drops to 9%, vs 25.9% for everyone else. But it’s no guarantee — several 600-upvote launches are gone without a trace.
  • Death comes fast: the 2025 cohort is already 20% dead after roughly a year.

In this report

  1. 1. The headline finding
  2. 2. Death rate by launch year
  3. 3. Does launch-day hype protect you?
  4. 4. AI vs everything else
  5. 5. The deadliest (and safest) categories
  6. 6. Notable casualties

  8. 8. What this means when you pick tools
  9. 9. Methodology
  10. 10. Download the dataset
  11. 11. FAQ

The headline finding

Every day, Product Hunt features roughly twenty new products — the launches that beat hundreds of others to the front page. These are not random side projects; they are the products that won attention, upvotes, and often press coverage on day one.

Plenty of curated “graveyard” lists exist — from Killed by AI to CB Insights’ startup post-mortems — but we couldn’t find anyone who had measured the actual rate: of the launches that win attention, how many still exist? So we took 59complete daily leaderboards spread across January 2023 – April 2025, found each product’s real website, and visited all 2,291 of them — as of July 18, 2026.

The answer: 24.4% are hard-dead (505 of 2,066products with a definitive verdict). Their domains fail DNS, sit parked with a “buy this domain” page, serve an expired security certificate, or 404 on their own homepage. Add the 4.5% whose domains now forward to some other company, and 28.9% of the products that won Product Hunt’s attention in the last three years no longer exist as independent products.

And this is the floor, not the ceiling: 9.8% of products gave ambiguous answers (anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings) and were excluded rather than counted — plus a site that still loads isn’t necessarily a business that still operates.

Death rate by launch year

Each row is the cohort of products that hit a sampled daily leaderboard in that year. “Dead” includes parked domains; “absorbed” (redirects to another company) is shown separately.

CohortVerifiedDeadAbsorbedDead % (AI only)
202379726.5%5.5%23.1%
202486024.7%3.7%26.5%
202540920%4.4%23.7%

Read the curve bottom-up: the 2025 cohort — barely a year old — is already 20% dead. The climb to 26.5% for the 2023 cohort means most products that die do so within their first year or two; survival past that point flattens the curve. If a tool you rely on launched recently, its riskiest period is right now.

Does launch-day hype protect you?

Partly — and this is the finding that surprised us most. We expected launch-day placement to be noise. It isn’t:

By finishing rank that day

Top 5 of the day9% dead
Rank 6 and below25.9% dead

By launch-day upvotes

Under 10026.4%
100–24923.3%
250–4997.1%
500+12.9%

A top-5 finish cuts the death rate roughly 2.9×. The likeliest explanation is not magic exposure — it’s selection: products capable of winning a competitive day tend to have real teams and real momentum behind them. But hype is a discount on risk, not insurance — the casualty table below is full of products that had huge launch days and are DNS-dead anyway.

AI vs everything else: no difference

The prevailing story says AI tools are uniquely ephemeral — thin wrappers doomed to die when the model underneath changes. Our data says otherwise: AI products are dead at 24.5%, non-AI at 24.4% (754 and 1,312 verified products respectively — a statistically meaningless gap).

One honest nuance: products carrying Product Hunt’s specific “Artificial Intelligence” topic tag die at 33.7% — above average. But so do comparable non-AI SaaS segments (see the category table below), and our broader AI definition — which also catches AI products filed under Writing, Developer Tools, or Marketing — lands exactly on the overall average. The boom changed what people build; it did not make software businesses die faster than they always have.

The deadliest — and safest — categories

Product Hunt topic tags with at least 40 verified products in the sample:

Highest death rate

Writing42 verified45.2%
Hiring40 verified40%
Artificial Intelligence86 verified33.7%
Knowledge base software45 verified33.3%
SaaS67 verified32.8%
Developer Tools41 verified29.3%

Lowest death rate

Open Source45 verified8.9%
iOS64 verified12.5%
Design resources40 verified15%
AI Generative Media48 verified18.8%
Fintech40 verified20%
Design Tools139 verified20.9%

Two patterns stand out. Writing tools are a killing field (45.2% dead) — the segment where a thin layer over a language model was easiest to ship and easiest to abandon. Open-source products barely die (8.9%): when the code is public, a project can outlive its founder’s attention span. If longevity matters to you, an open repository is the single strongest survival signal in this dataset.

Notable casualties

Each of these collected hundreds of upvotes on launch day. As of July 18, 2026, their domains fail DNS, sit parked, or serve expired certificates. (Paste any of them into the Wayback Machine to see what they looked like alive.)

ProductLaunchedUpvotesWhat’s there now

Butter 2.02023-02621Domain no longer exists
Humalect2023-04535Domain no longer exists
Belly Buddy2024-02463Broken security certificate — site abandoned
What to Meme2023-04412Domain no longer exists
YC Rejection2023-11398Domain no longer exists
Clearmind2023-08348Server no longer responds
Boeing Be Gone2024-07323Domain no longer exists
Devlopea 2.02025-02294Domain no longer exists

The absorbed: 94 products now redirect somewhere else

4.5% of verified products don’t 404 — their original domain quietly forwards somewhere else: an acquirer, a rebranded successor, or the founder’s next project. We don’t guess which from the outside — the dataset records the redirect, not the backstory. What’s certain is that the product as launched is gone from its original home. Live-verified examples:

  • Algomo (2023-01, 662 upvotes) — algomo.com now forwards to newmode.ai
  • AI Traffic Analytics (2023-09, 371 upvotes) — its link now forwards to writesonic.com (folded into the parent product)
  • Dashcam (2023-08, 366 upvotes) — dashcam.io now forwards to testdriver.ai
  • Breeew (2023-04, 280 upvotes) — breeew.com now forwards to getorchestra.com
  • Plus for Google Slides (2023-02, 254 upvotes) — plusdocs.com now forwards to plusai.com (rebrand)
  • Mermaid Whiteboard (2023-06, 246 upvotes) — mermaidchart.com now forwards to mermaid.ai (rebrand)

What this means when you pick tools

If you adopt a tool from a launch site today, this dataset says there’s roughly a 1-in-4 chance its website won’t exist in ~2 years — with your data, workflows, and integrations inside. Practical takeaways from the numbers:

  • Prefer products with an open-source core — the 8.9% death rate is the best survival signal we found.
  • Treat the first 12–24 months as the danger window — the 2025 cohort is already 20% dead. Demand an export path before you commit real workflows.
  • Weight evidence of a real business over launch-day noise: a top-5 finish helps (9% vs 25.9%), but pricing pages, changelogs and shipping cadence help more.
  • Check before you buy.This study exists because we track this continuously: we re-verify ~8,000 AI tools and score each one’s viability — including an at-risk list — precisely so you don’t build on a tool that’s about to join this page. Browse tools by category, compare them head-to-head, or see the broader State of AI Tools report this study belongs to.

Methodology — fully auditable

Sample. 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards drawn across January 2023 – April 2025 (~20 featured launches per day, all ranks — not just #1s). After de-duplicating relaunches (a product keeps its earliest appearance) and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products.

Probe (run July 18, 2026).Each product’s website was fetched with a standard browser user-agent, 15-second timeout, and retries with backoff. DNS was checked separately and double-checked to absorb resolver blips.

Verdicts. Dead = no DNS record, connection refused/reset, expired/invalid security certificate, or HTTP 404/410 at the domain level. Parked = a domain-parking sales page (counted with dead). Absorbed= the domain redirects to a different company’s domain; same-name domain moves count as alive. Unknown = anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings, and deep links that 404 while the root domain still responds — 225 products (9.8%), excluded from every percentage and never counted as dead.

A second, stricter pass. Before publishing we re-audited every 404-based verdict: products whose link pointed at an app-store listing or a deep page on a bigger site were re-probed at the domain root, and anything ambiguous was moved to unknown. This lowered the headline from a preliminary 27% to the published 24.4% — we’d rather under-claim than defend a soft number.

AI classification. A product counts as AI when Product Hunt tags it with an AI topic or its name/tagline/description contains AI terms (844 of 2,291 products).

Reproducibility. Before publishing, we re-probed the entire cohort a second time, end to end, hours after the first run. The headline reproduced exactly (24.4%), every reported statistic landed within ±0.6 points, and 99.4% of individual verdicts were identical — the remaining handful are sites flickering in real time, which is what the internet does.

Known limitations.A responding website is a proxy for an operating product — some “alive” sites are zombies with dead backends, so the true product death rate is likely higher than our headline. The sample covers featured (leaderboard) launches; unfeatured launches likely fare worse. The 2025 cohort covers sampled days through April 2025 only.

FAQ

How did you decide a product is "dead"?

Hard evidence only. We fetched every product's website with a normal browser user-agent. "Dead" means the domain no longer resolves in DNS, the connection is refused, the security certificate has expired, the homepage returns HTTP 404/410, or the domain is parked for sale. Anything ambiguous — anti-bot walls, timeouts, an app-store listing that vanished, a deep link that 404s while the company's main domain still works — is classed "unknown" and excluded from every percentage. Excluding the ambiguous cases can only make the true death rate higher than our headline, never lower.

Does a redirect count as dead?

Never in the headline. A product that moved domains but kept its name (deeto.ai → deeto.com) is counted alive — that's a migration, not a death. A product whose domain now forwards to a differently-named destination (algomo.com → newmode.ai) is reported separately as "absorbed" — that bucket includes acquisitions, rebrands, and founders pointing a dead product's domain at their next project. We record the redirect, not the backstory, and none of it counts toward the death rate.

What exactly is the sample?

Every product on 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards sampled between January 2023 and April 2025 — about 20 featured launches per day, every rank, not just the winners. After de-duplicating relaunches and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products, of which 2,066 produced a definitive verdict as of July 18, 2026. Each product's evidence line ships in the downloadable dataset, so any individual verdict can be audited.

Do AI products die faster than non-AI products?

Under our broad AI definition (AI topic tag on Product Hunt, or AI terms in the name/tagline/description) the rates are statistically identical: 24.5% for AI products vs 24.4% for everything else. One nuance worth knowing: products carrying Product Hunt's specific "Artificial Intelligence" topic tag die at 33.7% — the thin-wrapper era left real casualties — but comparable non-AI SaaS segments die at similar rates, and the broader AI population lands exactly on the overall average.

Does a big launch protect a product?

Partly, yes — and it surprised us. Products that finished in their day's top 5 are dead at only 9%, versus 25.9% for rank 6 and below. But success is no guarantee: several products with 500–700 launch-day upvotes now fail DNS entirely.

Why do so many top launches die?

The data can't prove motives, but the pattern in the casualties is consistent: single-purpose products built quickly on someone else's platform — browser extensions, one-feature "wrappers", weekend projects riding a trend — dominate the dead list, while open-source products die at only 8.9%, because a community can keep a project alive after its maker moves on. A launch spike is attention, not revenue.

Can I verify a specific product myself?

Yes — that's the point of shipping the evidence. Download the dataset below, find the product's row, and check the evidence column (the exact DNS error, redirect chain, or HTTP status we recorded). You can also paste any dead domain into the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine to see what the site looked like when it was alive.

Can I use this data?

Yes. The full per-product dataset (JSON and CSV, with evidence per verdict) is CC BY 4.0 — free to use, including commercially, with attribution. Cite "RightAIChoice — Product Hunt Graveyard study" with a link to this page.

Picking tools that will still exist next year?

We re-verify ~8,000 AI tools continuously and score their viability — that’s why we built this study.

See viability scores

sentences.en: Total gone as independent products: 28.9%.
sentences.zh: 作为独立产品消失的总数：28.9%。

blocks.html_en: • AI tools die at the same rate as everything else — 24.5% vs 24.4%. The AI boom changed what gets built, not what survives.

sentences.en: AI tools die at the same rate as everything else — 24.5% vs 24.4%.

sentences.en: The AI boom changed what gets built, not what survives.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的繁荣改变了建造的东西，而不是生存的东西。

blocks.html_en: • Launch-day success is real protection: finish in your day’s top 5 and the death rate drops to 9%, vs 25.9% for everyone else. But it’s no guarantee — several 600-upvote launches are gone without a trace.

sentences.en: Launch-day success is real protection: finish in your day’s top 5 and the death rate drops to 9%, vs 25.9% for everyone else.

sentences.zh: 但这并不能保证——几次获得 600 票赞成的发布都消失得无影无踪。

blocks.html_en: Every day, Product Hunt features roughly twenty new products — the launches that beat hundreds of others to the front page. These are not random side projects; they are the products that won attention, upvotes, and often press coverage on day one.

sentences.en: Every day, Product Hunt features roughly twenty new products — the launches that beat hundreds of others to the front page.
sentences.zh: Product Hunt 每天都会推出大约二十种新产品，这些新产品的发布击败了数百种其他产品，登上了首页。

sentences.en: These are not random side projects; they are the products that won attention, upvotes, and often press coverage on day one.
sentences.zh: 这些并不是随机的副业项目；它们是在第一天就赢得关注、好评并经常受到媒体报道的产品。

blocks.html_en: Plenty of curated “graveyard” lists exist — from Killed by AI to CB Insights’ startup post-mortems — but we couldn’t find anyone who had measured the actual rate: of the launches that win attention, how many still exist? So we took 59complete daily leaderboards spread across January 2023 – April 2025, found each product’s real website, and visited all 2,291 of them — as of July 18, 2026.

sentences.en: Plenty of curated “graveyard” lists exist — from Killed by AI to CB Insights’ startup post-mortems — but we couldn’t find anyone who had measured the actual rate: of the launches that win attention, how many still exist?
sentences.zh: 存在大量精心策划的“墓地”列表——从被 AI 杀死到 CB Insights 的初创公司事后剖析——但我们找不到任何人测量过实际比率：在赢得关注的发布中，有多少仍然存在？

sentences.en: So we took 59complete daily leaderboards spread across January 2023 – April 2025, found each product’s real website, and visited all 2,291 of them — as of July 18, 2026.
sentences.zh: 因此，截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日，我们收集了 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间的 59 个完整每日排行榜，找到了每个产品的真实网站，并访问了全部 2,291 个网站。

blocks.html_en: The answer: 24.4% are hard-dead (505 of 2,066products with a definitive verdict). Their domains fail DNS, sit parked with a “buy this domain” page, serve an expired security certificate, or 404 on their own homepage. Add the 4.5% whose domains now forward to some other company, and 28.9% of the products that won Product Hunt’s attention in the last three years no longer exist as independent products.

sentences.en: The answer: 24.4% are hard-dead (505 of 2,066products with a definitive verdict).
sentences.zh: 答案是：24.4% 是顽固分子（2,066 种产品中的 505 种有明确的结论）。

sentences.en: Their domains fail DNS, sit parked with a “buy this domain” page, serve an expired security certificate, or 404 on their own homepage.
sentences.zh: 他们的域名无法通过 DNS、停留在“购买此域名”页面、提供过期的安全证书或在自己的主页上出现 404。

sentences.en: Add the 4.5% whose domains now forward to some other company, and 28.9% of the products that won Product Hunt’s attention in the last three years no longer exist as independent products.
sentences.zh: 再加上 4.5% 的域名现在转发给其他公司，过去三年赢得 Product Hunt 关注的产品中有 28.9% 不再作为独立产品存在。

blocks.html_en: And this is the floor, not the ceiling: 9.8% of products gave ambiguous answers (anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings) and were excluded rather than counted — plus a site that still loads isn’t necessarily a business that still operates.

sentences.en: And this is the floor, not the ceiling: 9.8% of products gave ambiguous answers (anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings) and were excluded rather than counted — plus a site that still loads isn’t necessarily a business that still operates.
sentences.zh: 这是底线，而不是上限：9.8% 的产品给出了模棱两可的答案（反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表），并且被排除而不是被计算在内 - 而且仍然加载的网站不一定是仍在运营的业务。

blocks.html_en: Read the curve bottom-up: the 2025 cohort — barely a year old — is already 20% dead. The climb to 26.5% for the 2023 cohort means most products that die do so within their first year or two; survival past that point flattens the curve. If a tool you rely on launched recently, its riskiest period is right now.

blocks.html_en: A top-5 finish cuts the death rate roughly 2.9×. The likeliest explanation is not magic exposure — it’s selection: products capable of winning a competitive day tend to have real teams and real momentum behind them. But hype is a discount on risk, not insurance — the casualty table below is full of products that had huge launch days and are DNS-dead anyway.

sentences.en: A top-5 finish cuts the death rate roughly 2.9×.
sentences.zh: 排名前 5 名的死亡率可降低约 2.9 倍。

sentences.en: The likeliest explanation is not magic exposure — it’s selection: products capable of winning a competitive day tend to have real teams and real momentum behind them.
sentences.zh: 最可能的解释不是神奇的曝光——而是选择：能够赢得竞争的产品往往有真正的团队和真正的动力支持。

sentences.en: But hype is a discount on risk, not insurance — the casualty table below is full of products that had huge launch days and are DNS-dead anyway.
sentences.zh: 但炒作是对风险的折扣，而不是保险——下面的伤亡表充满了发布时间很长的产品，而且无论如何都是 DNS 死亡的。

sentences.en: AI vs everything else: no difference

blocks.html_en: The prevailing story says AI tools are uniquely ephemeral — thin wrappers doomed to die when the model underneath changes. Our data says otherwise: AI products are dead at 24.5%, non-AI at 24.4% (754 and 1,312 verified products respectively — a statistically meaningless gap).

sentences.en: The prevailing story says AI tools are uniquely ephemeral — thin wrappers doomed to die when the model underneath changes.
sentences.zh: 流行的说法是，人工智能工具的生命周期极其短暂——当底层模型发生变化时，薄薄的包装注定会消亡。

sentences.en: Our data says otherwise: AI products are dead at 24.5%, non-AI at 24.4% (754 and 1,312 verified products respectively — a statistically meaningless gap).
sentences.zh: 我们的数据却表明了相反的情况：人工智能产品的死亡率为 24.5%，非人工智能产品的死亡率为 24.4%（分别有 754 和 1,312 个经过验证的产品——这一差距在统计上毫无意义）。

blocks.html_en: One honest nuance: products carrying Product Hunt’s specific “Artificial Intelligence” topic tag die at 33.7% — above average. But so do comparable non-AI SaaS segments (see the category table below), and our broader AI definition — which also catches AI products filed under Writing, Developer Tools, or Marketing — lands exactly on the overall average. The boom changed what people build; it did not make software businesses die faster than they always have.

sentences.en: One honest nuance: products carrying Product Hunt’s specific “Artificial Intelligence” topic tag die at 33.7% — above average.
sentences.zh: 一个诚实的细微差别：带有 Product Hunt 特定“人工智能”主题标签的产品死亡率为 33.7%——高于平均水平。

sentences.en: But so do comparable non-AI SaaS segments (see the category table below), and our broader AI definition — which also catches AI products filed under Writing, Developer Tools, or Marketing — lands exactly on the overall average.
sentences.zh: 但类似的非 AI SaaS 细分市场也是如此（请参阅下面的类别表），而我们更广泛的 AI 定义（也包括在写作、开发人员工具或营销下归档的 AI 产品）恰好落在总体平均水平上。

sentences.en: The boom changed what people build; it did not make software businesses die faster than they always have.
sentences.zh: 繁荣改变了人们建造的东西；它并没有让软件企业比以往更快消亡。

blocks.html_en: Two patterns stand out. Writing tools are a killing field (45.2% dead) — the segment where a thin layer over a language model was easiest to ship and easiest to abandon. Open-source products barely die (8.9%): when the code is public, a project can outlive its founder’s attention span. If longevity matters to you, an open repository is the single strongest survival signal in this dataset.

blocks.html_en: If you adopt a tool from a launch site today, this dataset says there’s roughly a 1-in-4 chance its website won’t exist in ~2 years — with your data, workflows, and integrations inside. Practical takeaways from the numbers:

sentences.en: If you adopt a tool from a launch site today, this dataset says there’s roughly a 1-in-4 chance its website won’t exist in ~2 years — with your data, workflows, and integrations inside.
sentences.zh: 如果您今天采用发布网站上的工具，该数据集表示，其网站大约有四分之一的机会在大约 2 年内不存在——其中包含您的数据、工作流程和集成。

sentences.en: Practical takeaways from the numbers:
sentences.zh: 从这些数字中得出的实际结论：

blocks.html_en: • Prefer products with an open-source core — the 8.9% death rate is the best survival signal we found.

sentences.en: Prefer products with an open-source core — the 8.9% death rate is the best survival signal we found.
sentences.zh: 更喜欢具有开源核心的产品——8.9% 的死亡率是我们发现的最佳生存信号。

blocks.html_en: • Treat the first 12–24 months as the danger window — the 2025 cohort is already 20% dead. Demand an export path before you commit real workflows.

sentences.en: Treat the first 12–24 months as the danger window — the 2025 cohort is already 20% dead.
sentences.zh: 将前 12-24 个月视为危险窗口——2025 年的人群已经有 20% 死亡。

sentences.en: Demand an export path before you commit real workflows.
sentences.zh: 在提交实际工作流程之前要求导出路径。

blocks.html_en: • Weight evidence of a real business over launch-day noise: a top-5 finish helps (9% vs 25.9%), but pricing pages, changelogs and shipping cadence help more.

sentences.en: Weight evidence of a real business over launch-day noise: a top-5 finish helps (9% vs 25.9%), but pricing pages, changelogs and shipping cadence help more.
sentences.zh: 权重真实业务的证据而不是发布日的噪音：前 5 名的排名有帮助（9% vs 25.9%），但定价页面、变更日志和发货节奏更有帮助。

blocks.html_en: • Check before you buy.This study exists because we track this continuously: we re-verify ~8,000 AI tools and score each one’s viability — including an at-risk list — precisely so you don’t build on a tool that’s about to join this page. Browse tools by category, compare them head-to-head, or see the broader State of AI Tools report this study belongs to.

sentences.en: Check before you buy.This study exists because we track this continuously: we re-verify ~8,000 AI tools and score each one’s viability — including an at-risk list — precisely so you don’t build on a tool that’s about to join this page.
sentences.zh: 购买前请检查。这项研究的存在是因为我们不断跟踪这一点：我们重新验证了约 8,000 个人工智能工具，并对每个工具的可行性进行了评分（包括风险列表），这样您就不会在即将加入此页面的工具上进行构建。

sentences.en: Browse tools by category, compare them head-to-head, or see the broader State of AI Tools report this study belongs to.
sentences.zh: 按类别浏览工具，对它们进行正面比较，或者查看本研究所属的更广泛的人工智能工具状况报告。

sentences.en: Methodology — fully auditable
sentences.zh: 方法论——完全可审计

blocks.html_en: Sample. 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards drawn across January 2023 – April 2025 (~20 featured launches per day, all ranks — not just #1s). After de-duplicating relaunches (a product keeps its earliest appearance) and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products.

sentences.en: Sample.
sentences.zh: 样本。

sentences.en: 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards drawn across January 2023 – April 2025 (~20 featured launches per day, all ranks — not just #1s).
sentences.zh: 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间绘制的 59 个完整的 Product Hunt 每日排行榜（每天约 20 个特色发布，所有排名 - 不仅仅是排名第一）。

sentences.en: After de-duplicating relaunches (a product keeps its earliest appearance) and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products.
sentences.zh: 在重新启动去重复（产品保持其最早的外观）并删除没有可用网站链接的产品后，该队列中有 2,291 个产品。

blocks.html_en: Probe (run July 18, 2026).Each product’s website was fetched with a standard browser user-agent, 15-second timeout, and retries with backoff. DNS was checked separately and double-checked to absorb resolver blips.

sentences.en: Probe (run July 18, 2026).Each product’s website was fetched with a standard browser user-agent, 15-second timeout, and retries with backoff.
sentences.zh: 探测（2026 年 7 月 18 日运行）。每个产品的网站均使用标准浏览器用户代理、15 秒超时和回退重试来获取。

sentences.en: DNS was checked separately and double-checked to absorb resolver blips.
sentences.zh: DNS 进行了单独检查和双重检查，以吸收解析器的故障。

blocks.html_en: Verdicts. Dead = no DNS record, connection refused/reset, expired/invalid security certificate, or HTTP 404/410 at the domain level. Parked = a domain-parking sales page (counted with dead). Absorbed= the domain redirects to a different company’s domain; same-name domain moves count as alive. Unknown = anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings, and deep links that 404 while the root domain still responds — 225 products (9.8%), excluded from every percentage and never counted as dead.

sentences.en: Verdicts.
sentences.zh: 判决。

sentences.en: Dead = no DNS record, connection refused/reset, expired/invalid security certificate, or HTTP 404/410 at the domain level.
sentences.zh: 失效 = 无 DNS 记录、连接被拒绝/重置、安全证书过期/无效或域级别的 HTTP 404/410。

sentences.en: Parked = a domain-parking sales page (counted with dead).
sentences.zh: Parked = 域名停放销售页面（计入死亡页面）。

sentences.en: Absorbed= the domain redirects to a different company’s domain; same-name domain moves count as alive.
sentences.zh: Absorbed=域名重定向到不同公司的域名；同名域的移动算作有效。

sentences.en: Unknown = anti-bot walls, timeouts, vanished app-store listings, and deep links that 404 while the root domain still responds — 225 products (9.8%), excluded from every percentage and never counted as dead.
sentences.zh: 未知 = 反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表以及在根域仍然响应时 404 的深层链接 — 225 个产品 (9.8%)，被排除在每个百分比之外，并且从未算作死亡。

blocks.html_en: A second, stricter pass. Before publishing we re-audited every 404-based verdict: products whose link pointed at an app-store listing or a deep page on a bigger site were re-probed at the domain root, and anything ambiguous was moved to unknown. This lowered the headline from a preliminary 27% to the published 24.4% — we’d rather under-claim than defend a soft number.

sentences.en: A second, stricter pass.
sentences.zh: 第二次，更严格的通过。

sentences.en: Before publishing we re-audited every 404-based verdict: products whose link pointed at an app-store listing or a deep page on a bigger site were re-probed at the domain root, and anything ambiguous was moved to unknown.
sentences.zh: 在发布之前，我们重新审核了每个基于 404 的结论：链接指向应用程序商店列表或较大网站上的深层页面的产品会在域根重新探测，任何不明确的内容都会被移至未知。

sentences.en: This lowered the headline from a preliminary 27% to the published 24.4% — we’d rather under-claim than defend a soft number.
sentences.zh: 这将标题从最初的 27% 降低到了公布的 24.4%——我们宁愿低估也不愿捍卫一个软数字。

blocks.html_en: AI classification. A product counts as AI when Product Hunt tags it with an AI topic or its name/tagline/description contains AI terms (844 of 2,291 products).

sentences.en: AI classification.
sentences.zh: 人工智能分类。

sentences.en: A product counts as AI when Product Hunt tags it with an AI topic or its name/tagline/description contains AI terms (844 of 2,291 products).
sentences.zh: 当 Product Hunt 使用 AI 主题对其进行标记或其名称/标语/描述包含 AI 术语（2,291 个产品中的 844 个产品）时，该产品将被视为 AI。

blocks.html_en: Reproducibility. Before publishing, we re-probed the entire cohort a second time, end to end, hours after the first run. The headline reproduced exactly (24.4%), every reported statistic landed within ±0.6 points, and 99.4% of individual verdicts were identical — the remaining handful are sites flickering in real time, which is what the internet does.

sentences.en: Reproducibility.
sentences.zh: 再现性。

sentences.en: Before publishing, we re-probed the entire cohort a second time, end to end, hours after the first run.
sentences.zh: 在发布之前，我们在第一次运行数小时后对整个队列进行了第二次端到端的重新探测。

sentences.en: The headline reproduced exactly (24.4%), every reported statistic landed within ±0.6 points, and 99.4% of individual verdicts were identical — the remaining handful are sites flickering in real time, which is what the internet does.
sentences.zh: 标题准确地复制了（24.4%），每项报告的统计数据都在±0.6点之内，并且99.4%的个人判决是相同的——剩下的少数是实时闪烁的网站，这就是互联网的做法。

blocks.html_en: Known limitations.A responding website is a proxy for an operating product — some “alive” sites are zombies with dead backends, so the true product death rate is likely higher than our headline. The sample covers featured (leaderboard) launches; unfeatured launches likely fare worse. The 2025 cohort covers sampled days through April 2025 only.

sentences.en: Known limitations.A responding website is a proxy for an operating product — some “alive” sites are zombies with dead backends, so the true product death rate is likely higher than our headline.
sentences.zh: 已知的限制。响应的网站是正在运行的产品的代理——一些“活着”的网站是后端死掉的僵尸，所以真正的产品死亡率可能比我们的标题要高。

sentences.zh: 示例涵盖特色（排行榜）发布；没有特色的发布可能表现更糟。

sentences.en: The 2025 cohort covers sampled days through April 2025 only.
sentences.zh: 2025 年队列仅涵盖截至 2025 年 4 月的抽样日期。

blocks.html_en: How did you decide a product is "dead"?

sentences.en: How did you decide a product is "dead"?
sentences.zh: 您如何判断一个产品“已死”？

blocks.html_en: Hard evidence only. We fetched every product's website with a normal browser user-agent. "Dead" means the domain no longer resolves in DNS, the connection is refused, the security certificate has expired, the homepage returns HTTP 404/410, or the domain is parked for sale. Anything ambiguous — anti-bot walls, timeouts, an app-store listing that vanished, a deep link that 404s while the company's main domain still works — is classed "unknown" and excluded from every percentage. Excluding the ambiguous cases can only make the true death rate higher than our headline, never lower.

sentences.en: Hard evidence only.
sentences.zh: 只有确凿的证据。

sentences.en: We fetched every product's website with a normal browser user-agent.
sentences.zh: 我们使用普通的浏览器用户代理获取每个产品的网站。

sentences.en: "Dead" means the domain no longer resolves in DNS, the connection is refused, the security certificate has expired, the homepage returns HTTP 404/410, or the domain is parked for sale.
sentences.zh: “死亡”意味着域名不再在 DNS 中解析、连接被拒绝、安全证书已过期、主页返回 HTTP 404/410 或域名被停售。

sentences.en: Anything ambiguous — anti-bot walls, timeouts, an app-store listing that vanished, a deep link that 404s while the company's main domain still works — is classed "unknown" and excluded from every percentage.
sentences.zh: 任何模棱两可的东西——反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表、在公司主域仍然有效时 404 的深层链接——都被归类为“未知”，并被排除在所有百分比之外。

sentences.en: Excluding the ambiguous cases can only make the true death rate higher than our headline, never lower.
sentences.zh: 排除那些模棱两可的病例，只能让真实死亡率比我们的标题更高，永远不会更低。

sentences.en: Does a redirect count as dead?
sentences.zh: 重定向算死亡吗？

blocks.html_en: Never in the headline. A product that moved domains but kept its name (deeto.ai → deeto.com) is counted alive — that's a migration, not a death. A product whose domain now forwards to a differently-named destination (algomo.com → newmode.ai) is reported separately as "absorbed" — that bucket includes acquisitions, rebrands, and founders pointing a dead product's domain at their next project. We record the redirect, not the backstory, and none of it counts toward the death rate.

sentences.en: Never in the headline.
sentences.zh: 从来没有出现在标题中。

sentences.en: A product that moved domains but kept its name (deeto.ai → deeto.com) is counted alive — that's a migration, not a death.
sentences.zh: 移动域名但保留其名称（deeto.ai → deeto.com）的产品被视为存活 - 这是迁移，而不是死亡。

sentences.en: A product whose domain now forwards to a differently-named destination (algomo.com → newmode.ai) is reported separately as "absorbed" — that bucket includes acquisitions, rebrands, and founders pointing a dead product's domain at their next project.
sentences.zh: 域名现在转发到不同名称的目的地（algomo.com→newmode.ai）的产品被单独报告为“被吸收”——该范围包括收购、品牌重塑以及创始人将失效产品的域名指向他们的下一个项目。

sentences.en: We record the redirect, not the backstory, and none of it counts toward the death rate.
sentences.zh: 我们记录重定向，而不是背景故事，并且这些都不会计入死亡率。

sentences.en: What exactly is the sample?
sentences.zh: 样本到底是什么？

blocks.html_en: Every product on 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards sampled between January 2023 and April 2025 — about 20 featured launches per day, every rank, not just the winners. After de-duplicating relaunches and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products, of which 2,066 produced a definitive verdict as of July 18, 2026. Each product's evidence line ships in the downloadable dataset, so any individual verdict can be audited.

sentences.en: Every product on 59 complete Product Hunt daily leaderboards sampled between January 2023 and April 2025 — about 20 featured launches per day, every rank, not just the winners.
sentences.zh: 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间，对 59 个完整的 Product Hunt 每日排行榜上的所有产品进行了采样——每天大约有 20 个特色发布，每个排名，而不仅仅是获胜者。

sentences.en: After de-duplicating relaunches and dropping products with no usable website link, the cohort is 2,291 products, of which 2,066 produced a definitive verdict as of July 18, 2026.
sentences.zh: 在重新启动并删除没有可用网站链接的重复数据删除产品后，该队列共有 2,291 种产品，其中 2,066 种截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日做出了最终裁决。

sentences.en: Each product's evidence line ships in the downloadable dataset, so any individual verdict can be audited.
sentences.zh: 每个产品的证据线都包含在可下载的数据集中，因此可以审核任何单独的结论。

blocks.html_en: Do AI products die faster than non-AI products?

sentences.en: Do AI products die faster than non-AI products?
sentences.zh: AI产品比非AI产品消亡得更快吗？

blocks.html_en: Under our broad AI definition (AI topic tag on Product Hunt, or AI terms in the name/tagline/description) the rates are statistically identical: 24.5% for AI products vs 24.4% for everything else. One nuance worth knowing: products carrying Product Hunt's specific "Artificial Intelligence" topic tag die at 33.7% — the thin-wrapper era left real casualties — but comparable non-AI SaaS segments die at similar rates, and the broader AI population lands exactly on the overall average.

sentences.en: Under our broad AI definition (AI topic tag on Product Hunt, or AI terms in the name/tagline/description) the rates are statistically identical: 24.5% for AI products vs 24.4% for everything else.
sentences.zh: 根据我们广泛的人工智能定义（Product Hunt 上的人工智能主题标签，或名称/标语/描述中的人工智能术语），比率在统计上是相同的：人工智能产品为 24.5%，而其他产品为 24.4%。

sentences.en: One nuance worth knowing: products carrying Product Hunt's specific "Artificial Intelligence" topic tag die at 33.7% — the thin-wrapper era left real casualties — but comparable non-AI SaaS segments die at similar rates, and the broader AI population lands exactly on the overall average.
sentences.zh: 一个值得了解的细微差别：带有 Product Hunt 特定“人工智能”主题标签的产品死亡率为 33.7%（薄包装时代留下了真正的伤亡），但类似的非人工智能 SaaS 细分市场的死亡率相似，而且更广泛的人工智能群体正好处于总体平均水平。

blocks.html_en: Does a big launch protect a product?

sentences.en: Does a big launch protect a product?
sentences.zh: 大规模发布能保护产品吗？

blocks.html_en: Partly, yes — and it surprised us. Products that finished in their day's top 5 are dead at only 9%, versus 25.9% for rank 6 and below. But success is no guarantee: several products with 500–700 launch-day upvotes now fail DNS entirely.

sentences.en: Partly, yes — and it surprised us.
sentences.zh: 部分是的——这让我们感到惊讶。

sentences.en: Products that finished in their day's top 5 are dead at only 9%, versus 25.9% for rank 6 and below.
sentences.zh: 排在当天前 5 名的产品的死亡率仅为 9%，而排名 6 及以下的产品的死亡率为 25.9%。

sentences.en: But success is no guarantee: several products with 500–700 launch-day upvotes now fail DNS entirely.
sentences.zh: 但成功并不能保证：一些发布日获得 500-700 票赞成的产品现在完全无法通过 DNS。

blocks.html_en: Why do so many top launches die?

sentences.en: Why do so many top launches die?
sentences.zh: 为什么这么多顶级产品都夭折了？

blocks.html_en: The data can't prove motives, but the pattern in the casualties is consistent: single-purpose products built quickly on someone else's platform — browser extensions, one-feature "wrappers", weekend projects riding a trend — dominate the dead list, while open-source products die at only 8.9%, because a community can keep a project alive after its maker moves on. A launch spike is attention, not revenue.

sentences.en: The data can't prove motives, but the pattern in the casualties is consistent: single-purpose products built quickly on someone else's platform — browser extensions, one-feature "wrappers", weekend projects riding a trend — dominate the dead list, while open-source products die at only 8.9%, because a community can keep a project alive after its maker moves on.
sentences.zh: 数据不能证明动机，但伤亡的模式是一致的：在别人的平台上快速构建的单一用途产品——浏览器扩展、单一功能的“包装器”、顺应潮流的周末项目——在死亡名单中占据主导地位，而开源产品的死亡率只有 8.9%，因为社区可以在项目的创建者离开后让项目保持活力。

sentences.en: A launch spike is attention, not revenue.
sentences.zh: 发布高峰是关注度，而不是收入。

content_html_zh:
State of AI Tools/The Graveyard

RightAIChoice 的原创研究 · 每个网站都经过重新调查，截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日的判决 · 每个判决都带有可下载的证据

TL;DR — 截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日

  • 我们访问了包含 2,291 种产品的实时网站，这些产品在 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间跻身 Product Hunt 每日排行榜。
  • 24.4% 的人已经死了——域名不再解析、页面被停售、安全证书过期或主页返回 404。
  • 另外 4.5% 现在转向另一家公司（被收购或悄然倒闭）。 作为独立产品消失的总数：28.9%。
  • 人工智能工具的消亡率与其他工具相同——24.5% vs 24.4%。 人工智能的繁荣改变了建造的东西，而不是生存的东西。
  • 发布日的成功是真正的保护：进入当日前 5 名，死亡率降至 9%，而其他人的死亡率为 25.9%。 但这并不能保证——几次获得 600 票赞成的发布都消失得无影无踪。
  • 死亡来得很快：大约一年后，2025 年的队列中已有 20% 的人死亡。

标题发现

Product Hunt 每天都会推出大约二十种新产品，这些新产品的发布击败了数百种其他产品，登上了首页。 这些并不是随机的副业项目；它们是在第一天就赢得关注、好评并经常受到媒体报道的产品。

存在大量精心策划的“墓地”列表——从被 AI 杀死到 CB Insights 的初创公司事后剖析——但我们找不到任何人测量过实际比率：在赢得关注的发布中，有多少仍然存在？ 因此，截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日，我们收集了 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间的 59 个完整每日排行榜，找到了每个产品的真实网站，并访问了全部 2,291 个网站。

答案是：24.4% 是顽固分子（2,066 种产品中的 505 种有明确的结论）。 他们的域名无法通过 DNS、停留在“购买此域名”页面、提供过期的安全证书或在自己的主页上出现 404。 再加上 4.5% 的域名现在转发给其他公司，过去三年赢得 Product Hunt 关注的产品中有 28.9% 不再作为独立产品存在。

这是底线，而不是上限：9.8% 的产品给出了模棱两可的答案（反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表），并且被排除而不是被计算在内 - 而且仍然加载的网站不一定是仍在运营的业务。

自下而上地解读曲线：2025 年的群体——还不到一岁——已经有 20% 的死亡。 2023 年的这一数字攀升至 26.5%，这意味着大多数产品都会在上市后的一两年内消亡；超过该点的生存会使曲线变平。 如果您依赖的工具最近推出，那么它最危险的时期就是现在。

部分——这是最让我们惊讶的发现。 我们预计发布日的排名会很混乱。 它不是：

排名前 5 名的死亡率可降低约 2.9 倍。 最可能的解释不是神奇的曝光——而是选择：能够赢得竞争的产品往往有真正的团队和真正的动力支持。 但炒作是对风险的折扣，而不是保险——下面的伤亡表充满了发布时间很长的产品，而且无论如何都是 DNS 死亡的。

人工智能与其他一切：没有区别

流行的说法是，人工智能工具的生命周期极其短暂——当底层模型发生变化时，薄薄的包装注定会消亡。 我们的数据却表明了相反的情况：人工智能产品的死亡率为 24.5%，非人工智能产品的死亡率为 24.4%（分别有 754 和 1,312 个经过验证的产品——这一差距在统计上毫无意义）。

一个诚实的细微差别：带有 Product Hunt 特定“人工智能”主题标签的产品死亡率为 33.7%——高于平均水平。 但类似的非 AI SaaS 细分市场也是如此（请参阅下面的类别表），而我们更广泛的 AI 定义（也包括在写作、开发人员工具或营销下归档的 AI 产品）恰好落在总体平均水平上。 繁荣改变了人们建造的东西；它并没有让软件企业比以往更快消亡。

有两种模式很突出。 编写工具是一个杀戮场（45.2% 死亡）——语言模型上的薄层最容易发布也最容易放弃的部分。 开源产品几乎没有消亡（8.9%）：当代码公开时，一个项目的寿命可能会超过其创始人的注意力。 如果寿命对您很重要，那么开放存储库是该数据集中最强烈的生存信号。

被吸收：94 个产品现在重定向到其他地方

4.5% 的经过验证的产品没有 404——它们的原始域名悄悄转发到其他地方：收购者、重新命名的继任者或创始人的下一个项目。 我们不会从外部猜测——数据集记录了重定向，而不是背景故事。 可以肯定的是，推出的产品已经离开了原来的家。 实时验证的示例：

  • Algomo（2023-01，662 票）——algomo.com 现已转发至 newmode.ai
  • AI Traffic Analytics（2023-09，371 票）——其链接现在转发到 writesonic.com（合并到父产品中）
  • Dashcam（2023-08，366 票）— dashcam.io 现已转发至 testdriver.ai
  • Breeew (2023-04, 280 票赞成) — breeew.com 现已转发至 getorchestra.com
  • Plus for Google Slides（2023 年 2 月，254 票赞成）— plusdocs.com 现已转发至 plusai.com（品牌重塑）
  • Mermaid Whiteboard（2023 年 6 月，246 票赞成）— mermaidchart.com 现已转发至 mermaid.ai（品牌重塑）

当您选择工具时这意味着什么

如果您今天采用发布网站上的工具，该数据集表示，其网站大约有四分之一的机会在大约 2 年内不存在——其中包含您的数据、工作流程和集成。 从这些数字中得出的实际结论：

  • 更喜欢具有开源核心的产品——8.9% 的死亡率是我们发现的最佳生存信号。
  • 将前 12-24 个月视为危险窗口——2025 年的人群已经有 20% 死亡。 在提交实际工作流程之前要求导出路径。
  • 权重真实业务的证据而不是发布日的噪音：前 5 名的排名有帮助（9% vs 25.9%），但定价页面、变更日志和发货节奏更有帮助。
  • 购买前请检查。这项研究的存在是因为我们不断跟踪这一点：我们重新验证了约 8,000 个人工智能工具，并对每个工具的可行性进行了评分（包括风险列表），这样您就不会在即将加入此页面的工具上进行构建。 按类别浏览工具，对它们进行正面比较，或者查看本研究所属的更广泛的人工智能工具状况报告。

方法论——完全可审计

样本。 2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间绘制的 59 个完整的 Product Hunt 每日排行榜（每天约 20 个特色发布，所有排名 - 不仅仅是排名第一）。 在重新启动去重复（产品保持其最早的外观）并删除没有可用网站链接的产品后，该队列中有 2,291 个产品。

探测（2026 年 7 月 18 日运行）。每个产品的网站均使用标准浏览器用户代理、15 秒超时和回退重试来获取。 DNS 进行了单独检查和双重检查，以吸收解析器的故障。

判决。 失效 = 无 DNS 记录、连接被拒绝/重置、安全证书过期/无效或域级别的 HTTP 404/410。 Parked = 域名停放销售页面（计入死亡页面）。 Absorbed=域名重定向到不同公司的域名；同名域的移动算作有效。 未知 = 反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表以及在根域仍然响应时 404 的深层链接 — 225 个产品 (9.8%)，被排除在每个百分比之外，并且从未算作死亡。

第二次，更严格的通过。 在发布之前，我们重新审核了每个基于 404 的结论：链接指向应用程序商店列表或较大网站上的深层页面的产品会在域根重新探测，任何不明确的内容都会被移至未知。 这将标题从最初的 27% 降低到了公布的 24.4%——我们宁愿低估也不愿捍卫一个软数字。

人工智能分类。 当 Product Hunt 使用 AI 主题对其进行标记或其名称/标语/描述包含 AI 术语（2,291 个产品中的 844 个产品）时，该产品将被视为 AI。

再现性。 在发布之前，我们在第一次运行数小时后对整个队列进行了第二次端到端的重新探测。 标题准确地复制了（24.4%），每项报告的统计数据都在±0.6点之内，并且99.4%的个人判决是相同的——剩下的少数是实时闪烁的网站，这就是互联网的做法。

已知的限制。响应的网站是正在运行的产品的代理——一些“活着”的网站是后端死掉的僵尸，所以真正的产品死亡率可能比我们的标题要高。 示例涵盖特色（排行榜）发布；没有特色的发布可能表现更糟。 2025 年队列仅涵盖截至 2025 年 4 月的抽样日期。

FAQ

您如何判断一个产品“已死”？

只有确凿的证据。 我们使用普通的浏览器用户代理获取每个产品的网站。 “死亡”意味着域名不再在 DNS 中解析、连接被拒绝、安全证书已过期、主页返回 HTTP 404/410 或域名被停售。 任何模棱两可的东西——反机器人墙、超时、消失的应用程序商店列表、在公司主域仍然有效时 404 的深层链接——都被归类为“未知”，并被排除在所有百分比之外。 排除那些模棱两可的病例，只能让真实死亡率比我们的标题更高，永远不会更低。

重定向算死亡吗？

从来没有出现在标题中。 移动域名但保留其名称（deeto.ai → deeto.com）的产品被视为存活 - 这是迁移，而不是死亡。 域名现在转发到不同名称的目的地（algomo.com→newmode.ai）的产品被单独报告为“被吸收”——该范围包括收购、品牌重塑以及创始人将失效产品的域名指向他们的下一个项目。 我们记录重定向，而不是背景故事，并且这些都不会计入死亡率。

样本到底是什么？

2023 年 1 月至 2025 年 4 月期间，对 59 个完整的 Product Hunt 每日排行榜上的所有产品进行了采样——每天大约有 20 个特色发布，每个排名，而不仅仅是获胜者。 在重新启动并删除没有可用网站链接的重复数据删除产品后，该队列共有 2,291 种产品，其中 2,066 种截至 2026 年 7 月 18 日做出了最终裁决。 每个产品的证据线都包含在可下载的数据集中，因此可以审核任何单独的结论。

AI产品比非AI产品消亡得更快吗？

根据我们广泛的人工智能定义（Product Hunt 上的人工智能主题标签，或名称/标语/描述中的人工智能术语），比率在统计上是相同的：人工智能产品为 24.5%，而其他产品为 24.4%。 一个值得了解的细微差别：带有 Product Hunt 特定“人工智能”主题标签的产品死亡率为 33.7%（薄包装时代留下了真正的伤亡），但类似的非人工智能 SaaS 细分市场的死亡率相似，而且更广泛的人工智能群体正好处于总体平均水平。

大规模发布能保护产品吗？

部分是的——这让我们感到惊讶。 排在当天前 5 名的产品的死亡率仅为 9%，而排名 6 及以下的产品的死亡率为 25.9%。 但成功并不能保证：一些发布日获得 500-700 票赞成的产品现在完全无法通过 DNS。

为什么这么多顶级产品都夭折了？

数据不能证明动机，但伤亡的模式是一致的：在别人的平台上快速构建的单一用途产品——浏览器扩展、单一功能的“包装器”、顺应潮流的周末项目——在死亡名单中占据主导地位，而开源产品的死亡率只有 8.9%，因为社区可以在项目的创建者离开后让项目保持活力。 发布高峰是关注度，而不是收入。

我可以自己验证特定产品吗？

是的——这就是运送证据的目的。 下载下面的数据集，找到产品的行，然后检查证据列（我们记录的确切 DNS 错误、重定向链或 HTTP 状态）。 您还可以将任何失效域名粘贴到互联网档案馆的 Wayback Machine 中，以查看该网站在活动时的样子。

我可以使用这些数据吗？

是的。 每个产品的完整数据集（JSON 和 CSV，每个判决都有证据）是 CC BY 4.0 - 免费使用，包括商业使用，并注明归属。 引用“RightAIChoice — Product Hunt Graveyard 研究”并链接到此页面。
